title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a news quiz to test knowledge of recent Greater Boston stories.
The quiz includes topics like Dunkin', the World Cup, and the Patriots.
Peter Chianca, an editor since 2019, oversees general assignments and community quizzes.

title: Citywide needle pickup requests fluctuated from 2020 to 2025, but surged in these neighborhoods
author: Sana Muneer
bullets: Boston saw a 71% increase in needle pickup requests from 2022 to 2025, though requests remain 17% below 2020 levels.
Jamaica Plain experienced a 149% increase in needle reports from 235 in 2020 to 585 in 2025.
Fenway-Kenmore reported a 54% decline in needle incidents, marking the largest decrease among Boston neighborhoods over five years.

title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kaity Cimo from Scituate, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association.
Cimo honors her mother, who has Alzheimer's, through memories of their time cooking together.
The marathon effort highlights her mother's nurturing spirit, expressed through cooking and family care.

title: On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins lost 6-3 to the Nashville Predators, jeopardizing their playoff bid as the trade deadline nears.
Boston, currently a Wild Card team in the Eastern Conference, is only one point ahead of Columbus in the standings.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney considers roster moves at the trade deadline amidst team's inconsistent performance and playoff position.

title: Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge David Barron pauses deportation of Seamus Culleton, an Irish man detained in Texas, pending review of his case.
Culleton was arrested after incident at Home Depot and has been held for six months in an ICE detention camp.
Culleton, married to a U.S. citizen, has no recent U.S. criminal record but faces old drug-related charges in Ireland.

title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: George Schubert, 81, was identified as the victim of a house fire on Granite Street in Foxborough.
The fire, which began around 3 a.m. Tuesday, also killed Schubert's dog and caused significant damage.
Foxborough, Mansfield, and Plainville firefighters extinguished the blaze within an hour; the cause is under investigation.

title: Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's tree canopy expanded by 151 acres from 2019 to 2024, increasing citywide coverage to 28.5%.
East Boston, Jamaica Plain, South Boston, and West Roxbury saw the largest net gains in tree canopy.
The expansion supports Boston's climate goals, including reducing greenhouse emissions, as outlined in the 2030 Climate Action Plan.

title: Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Robert Kraft's group committed to cover $7.8 million in security costs for World Cup events at Gillette Stadium.
Foxborough officials remain unsatisfied, citing unresolved concerns with Boston Soccer 2026's financial plan for security funding.
Foxborough faces a March 17 deadline to decide on granting an event license for the World Cup matches.

title: Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Saints Tavern in Fenway is closing this weekend after nearly a decade of operation.
Rising insurance costs, especially liquor liability, led to the decision to close the bar.
The tavern, popular with Northeastern students, started as Our House East and was purchased in 2017.

title: 4 questions facing Celtics ahead of Jayson Tatum’s expected return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum is expected to return from an Achilles tendon injury for the Celtics' game against the Dallas Mavericks.
Tatum's return may alter Celtics' lineup and performance, as he regains form and chemistry with Jaylen Brown.
Coach Mazzulla must manage Tatum's minutes and impact on younger players like Baylor Scheierman and Hugo Gonzalez.
